formalization_risks: Division notation for FPS: need to define or use existing notation for a/c where c is invertible.

missing_or_uncertain_context: Exact Mathlib lemma for coefficient-wise equality of products given coefficient-wise equality up to n.

needed_mathlib_context.expected_signature_or_shape: theorem coeff_mul_eq_of_coeff_eq (a b c d : R⟦X⟧) (hab : ∀ m ≤ n, coeff m a = coeff m b) (hcd : ∀ m ≤ n, coeff m c = coeff m d) : ∀ m ≤ n, coeff m (a * c) = coeff m (b * d)
needed_mathlib_context.why_needed: To prove the product equality from coefficient-wise hypotheses.

needed_mathlib_context.expected_signature_or_shape: theorem coeff_inv_eq_of_coeff_eq (c d : R⟦X⟧) (hc : IsUnit c) (hd : IsUnit d) (hcd : ∀ m ≤ n, coeff m c = coeff m d) : ∀ m ≤ n, coeff m (c⁻¹) = coeff m (d⁻¹)
needed_mathlib_context.why_needed: To extend the equality to inverses, needed for division.

needed_project_context.name: xnEquiv_mul_of_coeff_eq
needed_project_context.why_needed: Provides the product equality lemma for FPS up to degree n.

planned_declarations.target_statement_sketch: For FPS a,b,c,d over a commutative ring K, with c,d invertible, if coefficients of a and b agree up to n, and coefficients of c and d agree up to n, then coefficients of a/c and b/d agree up to n.

units.source_focus_summary: Congruence lemma for division of FPS: coefficient-wise equality up to n of numerators and denominators implies coefficient-wise equality of quotients up to n.

formalization_risks: Triangular matrix definition: need to define lower/upper triangular in terms of matrix entries.

missing_or_uncertain_context: Exact Mathlib lemma for determinant of triangular matrix.

needed_mathlib_context.expected_signature_or_shape: theorem det_of_lowerTriangular (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K) (h : ∀ i j, i < j → A i j = 0) : A.det = ∏ i, A i i
needed_mathlib_context.why_needed: To compute determinant of lower triangular matrix as product of diagonal entries.

needed_mathlib_context.expected_signature_or_shape: theorem det_of_upperTriangular (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K) (h : ∀ i j, i > j → A i j = 0) : A.det = ∏ i, A i i
needed_mathlib_context.why_needed: To compute determinant of upper triangular matrix as product of diagonal entries.

planned_declarations.target_statement_sketch: For any triangular (lower or upper) matrix A over a commutative ring K, its determinant equals the product of its diagonal entries.

units.source_focus_summary: Determinant of triangular matrix equals product of diagonal entries.

formalization_risks: Uniqueness of inverse in a commutative ring: straightforward from definition.

needed_mathlib_context.expected_signature_or_shape: theorem IsInverse.unique (a b c : L) (h1 : IsInverse a b) (h2 : IsInverse a c) : b = c
needed_mathlib_context.why_needed: To prove at most one inverse.

needed_project_context.name: IsInverse
needed_project_context.why_needed: Definition of IsInverse used in the statement.

planned_declarations.target_statement_sketch: In a commutative ring L, any element a has at most one inverse.

units.source_focus_summary: Uniqueness of multiplicative inverse in a commutative ring.

formalization_risks: Vandermonde identity for natural numbers: standard combinatorial identity.

missing_or_uncertain_context: Exact Mathlib lemma for Vandermonde identity for natural numbers.

needed_mathlib_context.expected_signature_or_shape: theorem vandermonde (a b n : ℕ) : (a+b).choose n = ∑ k in range (n+1), (a.choose k) * (b.choose (n-k))
needed_mathlib_context.why_needed: To prove the Vandermonde identity for binomial coefficients.

planned_declarations.target_statement_sketch: For natural numbers a, b, n, the binomial coefficient (a+b choose n) equals the sum over k from 0 to n of (a choose k)*(b choose n-k).

units.source_focus_summary: Vandermonde identity for binomial coefficients over natural numbers.

formalization_risks: Bijection between partitions of length ≤ N and N-partitions: need to define both sets and the bijection.

missing_or_uncertain_context: Exact Mathlib lemma for bijection between partitions of length ≤ N and N-partitions.

needed_mathlib_context.expected_signature_or_shape: theorem NPartition_bijection : {λ : Nat.Partition | λ.length ≤ N} ≃ NPartition N
needed_mathlib_context.why_needed: To formalize the bijection stated in the proposition.

needed_project_context.name: toPartition
needed_project_context.why_needed: Existing function from NPartition to Nat.Partition.

planned_declarations.target_statement_sketch: There is a bijection between partitions of length ≤ N and N-partitions, given by padding with zeros.

units.source_focus_summary: Bijection between partitions of length ≤ N and N-partitions.
